content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Cool puzzle, instructions clear, not too complicated to understand or implement

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Well-framed problem with a neat balance of simplicity and logic, good for testing fundamental understanding.

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Nice parentheses puzzle, simple but not trivial.

Very cool concept! My only thoughts are about test cases.

I tried solving that using python, and my solution was totally identical to yours, except that in my first try it didn't occur to me that I needed two separate counters, and I only used one (I didn't use what you called "additions", and incremented/decremented only "open_needed" instead).

Of course the solution was wrong, but the only failing tests were validator 4 and 5, so if that were an actual clash of code I would have submitted my solution thinking I had solved it correctly.

I think that can be fixed by modifying one of the existing testcases, or adding a new one. For instance, swapping "validator 4" with "4 unbalanced" would fix that immediately.

I think that happened because your test cases are focused only on the number of parentheses, while they should also address the actual reasons for the unbalancing. Maybe try modifying them to reflect that, something like

Left imbalance:
INPUT: )(()())))()
OUTPUT: 3

Right imbalance:
INPUT: (()((()(
OUTPUT: 4

Inside out:
INPUT: )))()(()()))((
OUTPUT: 6

(I hope I counted correctly lol)

Hi
The default code usually gives hints on the type of the expected output, but here the stub generator generates code outputting '()' instead of an integer.

We have many clashes about balancing parentheses, but while this one is on the same theme, it actually requires a different task. Good job (unless I've overlooked a similar existing one)!

Goal
* Note that, like [[variables]], constants also have their own formatting syntax: {{constants}}.

For point 1, please format "(" and ")" as {{constants}}.

For point 2, please format "())" and "(()" as {{constants}}.

Input
* Please format "(" and ")" as {{constants}}.

Constraints
* Unless you plan to add cases with longer strings, please reduce the upper bound to 10.

Stub generator
* Please update the value 1000 here if the constraint is revised.

Cases
* The titles should use "unbalanced" instead of "balanced" to correctly describe the cases.

Thanks.
I have updated the contribution. If you have any objections, you can give me some more suggestions.
It's because I wrote it "()". Thanks for reminding me about this.

Thank you for the feedback. You've made a good point about the gap in test coverage.

Since the original author's account appears to be inactive (I'm not sure why), I went ahead and adjusted the test cases myself. Would you mind taking a look at the updated set if it feels more robust? If anything still seems missing, please feel free to share any additional cases you think should be included.
Thank you, that seems all correct now.
Thank you for the quick follow-up. I don't have further comments at the moment. 🙂
